Notice Of Motion And Motion Of Specially Appearing Defendant Douglass Manor Homeowners Association To Quash Service Of Summons On Fifth Amended Complaint
Off Calendar Jul-08-2020 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT DOUGLASS MANOR HOMEOWNERS' ASSOCIATION INC.'s Motion Quash Service Of Summons On Fifth Amended Complaint.

Defendant Douglas Manor Homeowner's Association's motion to quash is granted. On March 9, 2020, plaintiff added movant as Doe 1. The amendment was improper because the operative pleading (Fifth Amended Complaint) has no Doe allegations and plaintiff did not comply with Code of Civil Procedure 474. "It has long been the rule that an amended complaint that omits defendants named in the original complaint operates as a dismissal as to them." (Fireman's Fund Ins. Co. v. Sparks Construction, Inc. (2004) 114 Cal.App.4th 1135, 1142.) "Filing Doe amendments when the operative complaint contained no Doe allegations did not comply with Code of Civil Procedure section 474." (Fireman's Fund, 114 Cal.App.4th at 1144; see also McClatchy v. Coblentz, Patch, Duffy & Bass, LLP (2016) 247 Cal.App.4th 368, 375.)

The court rejects plaintiff's arguments. The court's orders, which were based on the mistaken belief that the operative complaint contained Doe allegations, do not cure the defect. Plaintiff fails to demonstrate that defendant "waived" the defect. Whether this action is ultimately consolidated with case #584726 and whether plaintiff properly served that action on defendant are issues that are not presently before the court. Plaintiff fails to demonstrate that defendant has made a general appearance in this action.

A proposed order is submitted by email.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman, Clerk: M. Goodman, Court Reporter: Anthony Vaughn, CSR# 6185, tonyvaughn@yahoo.com, Reported. =(302/EPS)

MOTION TO STRIKE 6TH AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Feb-05-2020 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Stephen Kavanaugh, Esq. 415-522-1100 Plaintiff in Pro Per.
Jessica Beeler, Esq. 415-438-5925 for Defendant Poalo Broggi.
Ryan Kraft, Esq. 510-550-8546 for Defendant Maurice Fakhoury.
Samantha Lewin, Esq. 415-777-5501 for Defendant Masterson through Court Call.
Lisa Roberts, Esq. 925-939-5330 x266 of Mcnamara, Ney, Betty, Slattery, Borges & Ambacher LLP for Defendant Tri State Enterprise through Court Call.

RULING - A R G U E D. The Court shares its views. Plaintiff argues. (as fully reported in the reporter's notes/transcript) The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT PAOLO BROGGI, MAURICE FAKHOURY's MOTION TO STRIKE PLAINTIFF'S 6TH AMENDED COMPLAINT.

Defendants' motion to strike plaintiff's sixth amended complaint is granted. There is no right to amend an amended complaint without leave of court. (Code Civ. Proc. 473, 473(a)(1); Hedwall v. PCMV, LLC (2018) 22 Cal.App.5th 564, 578-579.) The court's October 28, 2019 order granted plaintiff leave to amend the fifth amended verified complaint to add Douglass Manor Homeowner's Association as a defendant. Plaintiff did not seek, and the court did not grant, leave to file a further amended complaint making substantive changes to the existing factual allegations or causes of action. The sixth amended complaint therefore is stricken. (See Community Water Coalition v. Santa Cruz County Local Agency Formation Com. (2011) 200 Cal.App.4th 1317, 1329 [trial court correctly granted motion to strike new cause of action because it was "not drawn or filed in conformity with . . . an order of the court" within the meaning of Code of Civ. Proc. 436(b).].) The fifth amended verified complaint is the operative pleading in this action. Plaintiff may file an amendment to that complaint naming Douglass Manor Homeowner's Association as a Doe defendant.

Order is signed. (see scanned order for details)

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman, Clerk: M. Goodman, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR#8600, maria.torreano@gmail.com, Reported. =(302/EPS)

RULING - The matter is Uncontested. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT BRETT MASTERSON's MOTION TO STRIKE PORTIONS OF 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT.

Defendant Brett Masterson's motion to strike portions of plaintiff Stephen Kavanaugh's first amended complaint is denied as to the punitive damages allegations and granted without leave to amend as to the attorney's fees allegations. The battery claim is a sufficient predicate to support Mr. Kavanaugh's request for punitive damages. Mr. Kavanaugh's amended complaint does not identify any statutory, contractual, or legal basis for the recovery of attorney's fees. Moreover, since Mr. Kavanaugh is self-represented, he cannot recover fees. (Gutierrez v. G&M Oil Company, Inc. (2010) 184 Cal.App.4th 551, 561.)

DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jul-16-2018 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Stephen Kavanaugh 415-522-1100 Plaintiff in Pro Per.
No other Appearance.

RULING - The matter is Uncontested. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT BRETT MASTERSON's DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT.

Defendant Brett Masterson's demurrer to plaintiff Stephen Kavanaugh's first amended complaint is: a) overruled as to the first, fourth and sixth causes of action for negligence, intentional tort and negligence; b) sustained without leave to amend as to the second and third causes of action for premises liability and unfair business practice; and c) sustained with twenty days leave to amend as to the fifth cause of action for fraud. The issue of whether CCP 425.15 applies to the two negligence claims is not amenable to resolution on this demurrer. Section 425.15 provides that an order is necessary to sue "a person serving without compensation as a director or officer of a nonprofit corporation'" Mr. Masterson fails to identify any allegations or judicially noticeable facts that establish that (1) he is serving without compensation; and (2) the Douglas Manor Homeowners' Association is a nonprofit corporation. The premises liability claims fails because Mr. Kavanaugh does not allege that a dangerous condition of the property caused his alleged injuries. The unfair business practice claims fails because neither Mr. Masterson nor the Douglas Manor Homeowners' Association was engaged in a business practice that affected consumers or competitors. (Dinh Ton That v. Alders Maint. Assn. (2012) 206 Cal.App.4th 1419, 1427). The "intentional tort" claim is adequately alleged. The court construes that claim as a battery cause of action. Mr. Kavanaugh is granted leave to amend to allege his fraud claim with more particularity, notwithstanding that the fraud claim appears to be a restatement of the battery claim.

Order is signed. (see scanned order for details)

Judge: Harold Kahn, Clerk: M.Goodman, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR #8600, torreanom@aol.com, Reported. =(302/HK)
